floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: t/u round-trip is byte-real (target has same-slot lw/sw pairs) -> respell as setVector idiom; nested do-while(0) wrappers likely load-bearing sched fences (sanctionable w/ FAKE per

frontier.hypothesis: UNMEASURED: respell the t/u round-trip as the setVector comma-assign idiom (local[0]=local[0], local[1]=local[1]-0x384, local[2]=local[2] via a macro or direct comma-expr), leaving wrappers untouched - removes the flagged 'dead temp' spelling while keeping the byte-real self-copy stores
frontier.mechanism: none needed at codegen level - the self-copy lw/sw pairs are real target bytes; this is a pure source-honesty respell (era-idiomatic PsyQ setVector macro expansion)
frontier.next_probe: sandbox func_8001C624 --disable all with the setVector spelling substituted for the t/u block; expect distance 0 unchanged

frontier.hypothesis: UNMEASURED: wrapper inertness split-test - delete the inner do-while(0) and the outer while(0!=0) independently to classify each as inert vs load-bearing
frontier.mechanism: NOTE_INSN_LOOP_BEG/END emitted by the wrappers fence sched1's interleave (round-trip tail past the FDC..FF4 zero-store run) and/or bias RA ref weights
frontier.next_probe: two sandbox runs: (a) inner wrapper removed, (b) outer wrapper removed; any score rise = load-bearing -> retain that wrapper with inline FAKE annotation, normalize '0 != 0' to '0', and write the nested-wrap single-level-insufficient justification

frontier.hypothesis: UNMEASURED: natural-geometry wrapper-free variant - move the six zero-store statements D_80101FDC..D_80101FF4 between the setVector reads and its tail writes in source order, reproducing the target interleave without loop notes
frontier.mechanism: sched1 ready-list priority follows source/RTL order for same-cost insns; explicit source interleave may land the sw 0x18/0x14 placement that the wrappers currently force
frontier.next_probe: sandbox a variant with wrappers removed and the zero stores textually interleaved into the setVector expansion point

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Three distinct cheat constructs found — two unannotated do-while(0) wrappers (one with variant spelling `0 != 0`) and a dead load/store round-trip pair — all permuter-discovered, all framed in the commit by GCC-scheduling intent rather than program spec. Worker must redo the function in pure C, dropping the do-while wrappers and the t/u round-trips; the struct-copy Blk16/Blk12 pattern and the outer sequencing are clean and can stay.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)

Do NOT commit candidate.c's wrapper form. Next session: (1) write the s2 measurements into the DURABLE ledger — hypotheses.md and evidence.md entries and a state.json s2 floor_history entry, with the sandbox numbers, so exhaustion is auditable by someone other than the author; (2) work the three named frontier avenues (sched.c pending-list flush, whole-region struct typing in the header, directed permuter on the anchored base) to a documented dead end. The setVector respell and the `0 != 0` -> `0` normalisation are approved and may be carried forward. If and only if all three avenues are spent and recorded, resubmit a ruling request with the complete ledger — the nested wrappers can then be re-considered under do-while-zero-exception on their merits.
